body.title: Corpus verifiers reject CORRECT answers that carry a unit or natural phrasing
body.description:
FOUND 2026-07-09 by reading the raw replies behind a failure instead of trusting the verdict.

The corpus verifier (escalation_corpus.EvalQuery.verify) compares `normalize(extract_answer(reply)) == normalize(self.answer)` — exact equality on the normalized string. A model that answers CORRECTLY but phrases the answer naturally is scored as WRONG:

  b2-tank   ground truth '100'   deepseek-r1:32b said 'ANSWER: 100 litres'                -> scored fail
  b4-boxes  ground truth 'both'  deepseek-r1:32b said 'ANSWER: both apples and oranges'   -> scored fail

Both replies had correct step-by-step reasoning, finish_reason='stop', no truncation, and reproduced identically across 3 samples at temperature 0.

CONSEQUENCE: the first capability matrix produced by this instrument was WRONG. It reported deepseek-r1:32b (10/12) as strictly LESS capable than deepseek-r1:14b (11/12), including a b2 query the 32b 'failed'. That conclusion was an artifact of the verifier, not a property of the models. An instrument that under-reports capability makes every model look like it belongs on a lower rung — precisely the measurement the tier ladder is about to be rebuilt on.

WHY THE PROOF DID NOT CATCH IT: the closed ticket's proof was test_every_verifier_rejects_its_confabulation. That assertion is TRUE and remains true. It only ever constrained FALSE POSITIVES (accepting a wrong answer). Nothing constrained FALSE NEGATIVES (rejecting a right answer), so the verifier was free to be arbitrarily strict. A one-sided proof on a classifier proves one side.

WORK:
- Give each EvalQuery an explicit set of ACCEPTED phrasings (or an anchored regex the normalized extraction must fullmatch), covering the unit-bearing and natural-language forms a correct model actually emits: '100 litres', '100 L', 'both apples and oranges', '10 years old', 'Cy'.
- The accepted set must still REJECT the confabulation (fullmatch, never substring — 'the answer is not 42' must not verify as 42).
- Add the missing symmetric test: every query verifies each of its accepted phrasings. Both directions of the classifier get an assertion.
- Re-run escalation_matrix; the previous matrix is void. Do not carry its numbers forward.

**Test plan:** red->green, hermetic. RED: assert that q.verify('ANSWER: 100 litres') is True for b2-tank -> fails today. GREEN: every query accepts every declared phrasing AND still rejects its confabulation (the existing test keeps running unchanged — both sides asserted).

**Decision:** decision:D-domains-general-with-device-owned-specializations-2026-07-08

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: EvalQuery.accepts declares the phrasings a correct model actually emits; verify() compares against the accepted-forms SET (still equality, never substring). Proven: proofs/cc.0.corpus-verifier-accepts-correct-answers.T-escalation-corpus-false-negatives.20260709.084411352740.json (red=AssertionError -> green=passed). The rejects-its-confabulation test runs unchanged, so both directions of the classifier are asserted. The first capability matrix (which reported 32b < 14b) is VOID; re-running.
body.inferred_intention: modify the scoring mechanism for model responses
